---
title: "An Engineering Manual of Practice for Nature-Based Solutions"
authors: "S. Kyle McKay, Emily Corwin, and Troy Dorman"
Date: "June 2026"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
\documentclass: book
output:
  bookdown::bs4_book: default
github-repo: skmckay/NBS.Manual.Of.Practice
---

<span style="color: red;">ALL CONTENT IN THIS REPORT IS PRELIMINARY AND DRAFT. DO NOT REFERENCE FOR ANY PURPOSE.</span>

# Preface {-}

## What is this document? {-}

Introduce NBS.  
Introduce ASCE.  
Describe a manual of practice.  

## Points of contact {-}

The editors of this manual are: 

S. Kyle McKay, Ph.D., P.E.  
Environmental Director, Woolpert  
kyle.mckay at woolpert.com
New York, New York

Emily Corwin, M.S., P.E.  
Interim Executive Director, San Francisco Estuary Institute
emilyc at sfei.org  
Richmond, California

Tory Dorman, Ph.D., P.E., CFM, ENV SP  
Vice President, Director of Water Resources and Sustainability, Halff  
tdorman at halff.com
San Antonio, Texas

The ASCE points of contact are:  

Brian Parsons  
Managing Director, Environmental Water Resources Institute & Chief Sustainability Officer, American Society of Civil Engineers  
bparsons at asce.org  
Washington, D.C.

Ruth Hocker, P.E.  
Director, Technical Community (EWRI/COPRI/CTA), American Society of Civil Engineers  
RHocker at asce.org  
City, State

```{r options, include=FALSE}
#Markdown options
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message = FALSE) 

##########
#Clear local memory
rm(list=ls(all=TRUE))

##########
#Load R packages
library(bookdown)
library(downlit)
library(viridis) #Color palette
library(tidyr) #Data wrangling
library(dplyr) #Data manipulation
library(ggplot2) #plotting commands
library(gridExtra) #Arranging figures
library(scales) #visualization stuff
library(kableExtra) #Tables for markdown
library(stringr) #Manipulate strings

##########
#SET COLOR PALETTES
AOC.col <- "#002E5D"
AOC.col1 <- c("#A0291D", "#EB7A28", "#E39D1B", "#FFDB09", "#68954F", "#006955", "#47857D", "#006171", "#5BB6E7", "#4E4A84")  
AOC.col2 <- c("#C2705F", "#F9A35F", "#FCB21B", "#FFEDB2", "#A8CC95", "#96D4CC", "#93BFBA", "#8CC1CD", "#BCE2F5", "#C4C1DF")  
AOC.col3 <- c("#7E6A56", "#552318", "#AA534A", "#61524E", "#EEE1C3", "#E0C5B4", "#D5A897", "#CCC8C4")
```
